title: Table Saw Router Table Wing
summary: Save TONS of valuable space in your shop by inserting a router lift into the extension wing of your table saw
description: Save TONS of valuable space in your shop by inserting a router lift into the extension wing of your table saw

materials.name: Box of 1 ¼” pocket hole screws (See tools list)

steps.title: Cut plywood
steps.body: Cut all pieces according to the cutlist. Note that all of the pieces on this project are made out of ¾” plywood.

steps.title: Attach side pieces together
steps.body: Using pocket holes, attach all of the side pieces together securely.

steps.title: Attach side assembly to top
steps.body: Using pocket holes, attach the side assembly to the top.

steps.title: Cut out router plate
steps.body: After determining where you want the router plate, cut out the shape with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Attach router plate levelers
steps.body: Secure router plate levelers to the bottom of the router table to ensure router is secure.

steps.title: Apply durable finish
steps.body: Apply a durable finish like epoxy or polyurethane to help protect the router table from bumps and bruises over time.

steps.title: Wax table
steps.body: To ensure that pieces slide nicely on the router table, apply a simple wax coating and you are finished!
